ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Schofield, via Zoom, for Daniel Cunningham, Trustee; Trisha Friedeberg, via Zoom, for Julie Menack, Conservator of the Person.

The court advises Ms. Schofield that the court believes it is premature to terminate the trust at this time and that it will be requiring an accounting before terminating the trust. The court and counsel discuss the pending fee petitions set for Jul-11-2022 in the conservatorship matter.

After additional discussion the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Schofield, via ZOOM, counsel for Daniel Cunningham, Trustee; Christopher Jew, court-appointed counsel for Norman Petersen; Conservatee.

At the outset, the court asks Mr. Jew if he accepts less than thirty day notice as it appears that the proof of service filed with the court regarding this hearing shows a service date of May 6, 2021. Mr. Jew waives notice. The court then asks Mr. Jew if he is amenable to continuing with the hearing without his client present. Mr. Jew reports that Mr. Petersen had checked-in via ZOOM but that he was no longer present, but is willing to address the court regardless. Mr. Jew reports that his client has basically objected to every court related matter. The court then turns to Ms. Schofield regarding the $3000 worth of upgrades that have been paid to a company owned by a friend of Mr. Petersen's former spouse. Ms. Schofield reports that due to the contentious nature of their relationship, that money was paid for work that was required prior to selling real estate but that the costs had been shared between the former spouse and the Trust. The court then turns to Ms. Schofield regarding whether she plans to move to biennial accounting, Ms. Schofield confirms that the plan is to move to biennial accounting. Then the court queries Ms. Schofield regarding a plan to terminate the conservatorship and discharge Mr. Cunningham. Ms. Schofield reports that at the moment, she'd prefer to keep the conservatorship and file waivers of accounting and for a bond to remain in place.

After additional discussion, the Accounting is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Schofield appeared by zoom for Daniel Cunningham (Trustee).

Ms. Schofield stated that her Client is working on the accounting, noted that we are almost at the end of 2020 and asked the Court if it would be open to making it a two-year accounting. The Court denied Ms. Schofield's request. Ms. Schofield requested a 30-day continuance. Status on filing of the second accounting is continued to December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition is to be filed by November 25, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Granted Per Written Order

* ERROR *
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
This matter was called with Case No. PCN-11-294225; Conservatorship of Norman Lloyd Petersen.

Appearances: Kathryn Schofield appearing by Zoom for Daniel J. Cunningham (Successor Conservator of the Estate/Trustee). Trisha Friedeberg appearing by Zoom for Julie Menack.

Ms. Friedeberg requested a two week continuance of the Filing of the Status Report stating that they were waiting on information from the care home. The Court continued today's status hearing in the conservatorship to August 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Court addressed the issues in the trust with regard to a payment made to counsel without a court order. Ms. Schofield stated that she remembered receiving a check, that she knows that counsel is not to be paid without a court order, and that she understands that the payment she recieved will be deducted from their current fee request. The Court then addressed bond. Ms. Schofield stated that they have an ex parte, that it should happen next week, and that the paperwork is with the bond company. The Court stated that the accounting will not be approved without the new bond. Ms. Schofield stated that if the Court did not have a proposed order that she would submit one prior to the next hearing should the petition be pre-granted. The Court continued the Amended First Account and Report By Trustee; Request For Fees in the trust matter to August 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on bond is also set for that date and time in the trust.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Terms Of Private Sale
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-294225 Conservatorship of Norman Lloyd Petersen and
PTR-18-301525 The Norman Lloyd Petersen Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Kathryn Schofield for and with Daniel Cunningham (Petitioner).
Richard Grabstein for and with Floriana Petersen (Buyer).
Caroline Hinshaw appeared by telephone for Lloyd Petersen.

The Court questions why the sale shouldn't go through the usual Court bidding process, raised the issue of the difference in the amounts of the appraisals and asked whether the Appraisers talked to each other. Ms. Schofield stated that there were two appraisals, that everybody agrees this is what Mr. Petersen would want and that Mr. Petersen wants to keep it within the most economical form. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Mr. Petersen appreciates what is going on with the sale, that he wants the property to go to Floriana so that it could eventually go to Adriana and that she is satisfied with the negotiation process that has gone on. Ms. Schofield stated that her Client got the first appraisal and then Mr. Petersen, that there was deep conversation as to the appropriateness of both appraisals. Mr. Grabstein stated that the ultimate price is a compromise as to what needs to be done to the property. Ms. Schofield spoke of what was taken into consideration. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she was copied on the e-mails and that she is fully satisfied that her Client is accomplishing his goal if the property goes to Floriana. Ms. Schofield addressed fees stating that this is the most complicated conservatorship she has, that there was an issue of having to recalculate bond and on what, that there was a lot more activity involved as to what petitions should be brought and that they spent an enormous amount of time looking at Mr. Petersen's financial situation. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she has no objections to the fee request. The Second and Final Amended Account and Report by Conservator, etc. and the Petition for Approval of Terms of Private Sale are under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
